content.text: JavaScript arrays are ordered, zero-indexed collections that can hold any mix of types. They come with a rich set of built-in methods split into two categories: those that
the original array and those that
. Knowing which is which prevents subtle bugs in Angular components and RxJS pipelines.

attrs.alt: JavaScript arrays — structure, mutation vs. non-mutation, core higher-order methods
attrs.title: JavaScript arrays — structure, mutation vs. non-mutation, core higher-order methods

content.text:
const arr = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd'];

arr[0];           // 'a'
arr[arr.length - 1];  // 'd'  (last — old way)
arr.at(-1);       // 'd'  (ES2022 — cleaner)
arr.at(-2);       // 'c'

// Out-of-bounds returns undefined, never throws
arr[99];          // undefined
These methods change the array in place. In Angular,
avoid mutating arrays stored in component state or signals
— create a new array instead to trigger change detection.
reduce — accumulate to a single value

@for (item of items(); track item.id) { }
  • {{ item.name }}
Sorting the original array — always spread first: [...arr].sort(...) or use toSorted()
Mutating a signal array in place — signal() won't detect the change; use update() with a new array
Using == to compare arrays — arrays are objects; [1,2] == [1,2] is false (different references)
Calling .map/.filter inside a template expression — compute once in a signal or pipe, not on every render
Forgetting that Array(5) creates holes, not 5 undefineds — use Array.from({length:5}, ...) instead
Array destructuring extracts elements by position into named variables. It composes well with function returns and iterables.
For numeric data (image processing, audio, WebGL), JavaScript provides typed arrays that store values in contiguous memory — far faster than regular arrays for large datasets.
Because each non-mutating method returns a new array, they can be chained together in a pipeline. This is a common pattern in Angular services.
Angular unit tests frequently assert array state. Jest and Jasmine both have matchers for arrays.
JavaScript arrays are ordered reference-type collections with two classes of methods: mutating (
). In Angular, always produce a new array when updating state in signals or with OnPush change detection — spread (
), map, or filter to get a fresh reference. Use
loops to help Angular reuse DOM nodes efficiently.
